channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Brie Larson Thinks Hollywood Can Learn From Gaming Community
item.content:encoded:
Brie Larson believes it’s time for the film and TV universe to consider what they could learn from the world of video games.

Brie Larson is a proud gamer. The actress, known for her role as Captain Marvel in the Marvel Cinematic Universe, is voicing Rosalina in The Super Mario Galaxy Movie. She’s often talked about her love for video games, and now she’s explaining what she’s gleaned from conversations with those who work in the gaming industry.

It’s Time for Cinema to See What It Can Take From Games, Says Brie Larson

Speaking on Fandango’s Seen on the Screen podcast, Larson explained that she believes the world of gaming is larger than the world of TV and film. She also says she’s been talking more with those who work in video game companies and believes that, just as people ask about the films, shows, and books their peers are interested in, video games should be included in that conversation too.

Furthermore, she believes that video games have taken a lot from Hollywood, and now it’s time for Hollywood to learn from the video game medium.

“The gaming community is much bigger than the film and television world… People: ‘What movies did you see?’ or ‘What book are you reading?’, but not so much to ask, ‘What video game are you playing right now?’ I believe we’d be surprised to find that more people are actually playing games,” she says. “There’s so much that video games are taking from cinema, and I think it’s really time for us in cinema to recognize what we can take from video games.”

More on The Super Mario Galaxy Movie

Not only will Brie Larson be featured as Rosalina in the upcoming Mario movie, but Donald Glover will be playing Yoshi. Issa Rae will also be in the movie as the Honey Queen. Also, it has been announced that Star Fox’s Fox McCloud will be in the film. However, his voice actor has not yet been revealed.

The movie will be released on April 1, 2026. Insider Gaming will continue to provide updates on the movie leading up to its release.

What do you think about these comments made by Brie Larson? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: NYT Strands #753 Hints and Answers for Today, March 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #752 for Thursday, March 26, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, March 26, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #753 are all wind instruments. So, try to think of middle school band class, get some air in your lungs, and get ready to play.

General Strands Hints For March 26

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Squidward’s prized possession.
  • Hint 2: This is also a name for a device a spy might use.
  • Hint 3: A common instrument featured in blues and country songs.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Strands #753 for March 26, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for March 26, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • CREAM
  • TOON
  • WIND
  • STRUM
  • HARM

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #753 On March 26, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • HARMONICA
  • RECORDER
  • CLARINET
  • FLUTE
  • OBOE
  • SPANAGRAM: WINDINSTRUMENT

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #753?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on March 27 will be Strands Game #754.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-strands-753-hints-and-answers-for-today-march-26-2026/

item.title: The Eternal Life of Goldman Releasing on Xbox in 2026, Demo Out Now
item.content:encoded:
The Eternal Life of Goldman is an upcoming platformer from THQ Nordic with a whimsical art style, magical creatures, and a story that seems to center around immortality. The trailer narration notes that, as Goldman, you are tasked with saving the inhabitants of The Archipelago, and perhaps they aren’t thrilled about their fate being in the hands of a silly elderly man.

The game was revealed during the March 2026 Xbox Partner Preview event. The game is currently slated for release later this year, with a demo available now.

The trailer showcased several of the creatures players will encounter, updates to his Cane, and more. It ends with the ominous line that some are so old that “they forget to die.” There are also hints, but perhaps the journey you’re going through is representative of the tall tales that some elderly individuals share about their lives.

More on The Eternal Life of Goldman

Per the write-up on Xbox Wire, the game will task players with killing “The Deity,” a mysterious creature known to everyone but never seen. Goldman will be venturing across different landscapes, and each area is touted as being crafted and colored by classic frame-by-frame techniques. Check out the full synopsis below:

“Jump into a whimsical platformer coming to Xbox later this year. As the titular Goldman, you’ll explore a magical, hand-drawn world inspired by fairytales and myths. Your mission is to kill The Deity, a mysterious, fearsome creature that everyone knows about, but no one has ever seen. Equipped with his trusty cane and a unique host of abilities, Goldman must adventure across this vibrant landscape, each area and level lovingly crafted and colored by hand using classic frame-by-frame techniques.

“The Eternal Life of Goldman launches in 2026 for Xbox Series X|S, Xbox on PC, and Xbox Cloud. It will be an Xbox Play Anywhere title and will be available with Xbox Game Pass. Even better – a demo is available today.”

Insider Gaming will continue to provide the latest news on The Eternal Life of Goldman as details are announced. Check out everything being announced at the current Xbox Partner Preview Event here.

How do you feel about The Eternal Life of Goldman? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Ubisoft’s Cost-Cutting Crisis Could Put Its Biggest Games at Risk
item.content:encoded:
On the morning of March 19, Ubisoft’s Chief Studios and Portfolio Officer, Marie-Sophie de Waubert, sent a company-wide email to staff announcing that Ubisoft would stop game development at Red Storm Entertainment, resulting in the layoff of 105 employees.

The email stated that the decision was part of Ubisoft’s global cost-reduction plan and that the “decision follows a careful review of our operational and strategic priorities in the challenging content we’re navigating through.” Less than 24 hours later, teams were scrambling to remedy the fallout from this “careful review” to keep what Ubisoft has deemed some of its critical game releases on track.

One such release is the next mainline Ghost Recon game, codenamed OVR, set to take the franchise back to its roots as a first-person tactical shooter with a single-player and multiplayer component.

According to sources familiar with Ubisoft’s plans, OVR is currently considered a “crucial release” for Ubisoft’s upcoming fiscal year, alongside the slated summer release of the yet-to-be-announced Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Remake.

Yet, despite the game being internally marked as critical to Ubisoft’s success, OVR has already been scaled back considerably from its original plans, with those working on it fearing that the latest closure of Red Storm Entertainment’s game development division could lead to further descoping or delays. That’s because dozens of people at Red Storm were working on the project, and the void that they will leave will be difficult to fill with the right people.

Those affected by last week’s layoffs at Red Storm are currently protected under the Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act (WARN Act), which requires employers to give employees 60 days’ notice of planned closings and/or mass layoffs of more than 100 employees. Even though their final day is set for May 18th, sources say affected employees had their access to Ubisoft’s systems revoked immediately after the announcement. Some employees were then asked the next day to return to their projects during the WARN period, who were mainly those working on OVR and a Watch Dogs Legion director’s cut.

At the time of Red Storm Entertainment’s gaming division’s closure, the team was working on approximately 10 projects at Ubisoft, including the hotly anticipated Splinter Cell Remake – another game at Ubisoft that has had its issues and was said to be well over budget, according to people familiar.

Red Storm’s gaming division’s closure is the latest example of how Ubisoft’s wholesale policy of saving money by shuttering entire studios could lead to more problems, and, unfortunately, it’s hard to see a path forward. Red Storm was in Ubisoft’s new ‘Creative Network’, but it was fundamental in keeping some projects on track, just like the other 19 studios support studios.

A Sticky Situation

Layoffs at any studio are devastating for those involved, and with Ubisoft still planning further cost-cutting measures totaling another 100 million euros as its “third and final” implementation, thousands more layoffs at the French publisher are likely yet to come.

Speaking to dozens of current and former Ubisoft employees over the last couple of months, the consensus is that the studio-targeted closures and layoffs are doing more harm than good for Ubisoft and its games. As much as layoffs suck, those I spoke to argued that the layoffs don’t feel targeted. Instead, Ubisoft seems to look at a studio, its costs, and then pull the trigger without any foresight into how it could affect projects in its pipeline.

Those I spoke to argued that Ubisoft still needs to start from the top down, aiming to assign the right people to the right jobs.

Unfortunately, all of this seems like a lose-lose situation for Ubisoft. They need to save money, but refuse to look at the root cause, or at the very least, are in denial about what is causing the issues in the first place. Studio closures on a mass scale are creating voids in projects, leading to further delays, scope reductions, and higher budgets – the very thing that got them in this mess in the first place.

It’s almost like lessons aren’t being learned, but is that surprising at this point?

Ubisoft was asked for comment on all of the above and, at the time of publication, had not responded.

Both current and former employees can contact me securely and anonymously at [email protected] if you would like to weigh in on current events or share your story.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that PS3 consoles just got an update.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/ubisofts-cost-cutting-measures-will-give-us-worse-games/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Moosa: Dirty Fate Revealed During Xbox Partner Preview
item.content:encoded:
Moosa: Dirty Fate, an upcoming action game set in feudal Korea, was revealed during the March 2026 Xbox Partner Preview event. The game is currently slated for release in 2027.

The trailer showcased some third-person combat, followed by scenes of fire and a barren wasteland where the main protagonist presumably has roots.

More on Moosa: Dirty Fate

Per the write-up on Xbox Wire, the game features an unknown, powerful force that has swept across the protagonist’s land, leaving crops turning to dust. It said that the settings inhabitants have turned to the most “extreme methods of survival,” and it will be up to the player to track down the threat and save their people. Check out the full synopsis below:

“We saw another world premiere in Moosa: Dirty Fate, an upcoming action title set in Feudal Korea. An unknown, powerful force has swept your land, and with fields and crops withering to dust, the people have turned to the most extreme methods of survival. It’s up to you to track down the threat and save your subjects.

“Painterly Korean-inspired landscapes paired with slick third-person combat gave us a first glimpse into Moosa: Dirty Fate, coming in 2027 for Xbox Series X|S, Xbox on PC, and Xbox Cloud. It will be an Xbox Play Anywhere title and available with Xbox Game Pass.”

Insider Gaming will continue to provide the latest news on Moosa as details are announced. Check out everything being announced at the current Xbox Partner Preview Event here.

How do you feel about Moosa: Dirty Fate? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Brutal New LoL MSI Play-In Will Eliminate Three Major Teams
item.content:encoded:
First Stand recently concluded, and esports fans are already looking forward to the next international League of Legends tournament, the Mid-Season Invitational (MSI).

Riot Games recently announced new details about this year’s LoL MSI and Worlds events, and one major change has gone under the radar.

The event, which invites the top two teams from every region (apart from Brazil), will this year have a very unforgiving Play-In Stage.

Only Two 2nd Seeds Will Make the LoL MSI Main Stage

Now that Riot Games has split the Americas again into the LCS and CBLOL, the format for LoL MSI 2026 has changed slightly.

Last year, two teams were able to qualify from the LoL MSI Play-Ins: this year’s First Stand finalists, G2 Esports and Bilibili Gaming.

However, following the Americas split, Brazil’s sole representative will automatically qualify for the LoL MSI 2026 Bracket Stage.

And because of this, the 2026 Play-Ins will only allow one team to qualify for the remainder of the tournament.

Competition is going to be fierce as the second seeds from the LEC, LCK, LCS, and LCP will battle it out against each other, with only one progressing in the tournament.

Those four teams will compete in a double-elimination bracket, lasting over three rounds, to decide who will be the sole qualifier.

Meanwhile, the first seeds from every region and the second seed from LoL First Stand’s highest-ranking region will automatically start in the Bracket Stage.

Both LPL teams will skip this year’s LoL MSI Play-Ins, thanks to Bilibili Gaming’s first international title win and G2’s heroic run against LCK teams.

Suddenly, the Spring Splits for most regions just became even more intense, as it will now be even more important for a team to win their respective region.

What do you think about these MSI format changes? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And after failing to make First Stand, all eyes will be on world champions T1 without coach kkOma to see if they can qualify for MSI.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/brutal-new-lol-msi-play-in-will-eliminate-three-major-teams/

item.title: NACON Cancels Physical Releases for Multiple Upcoming Games
item.content:encoded:
NACON has decided to cancel multiple physical releases of upcoming games, a retailer claims.

According to Video Games Plus, the outlet described as North America’s premier independent videogame retailer/distributor, physical releases for Dragonkin, Cthulhu: The Cosmic Abyss, Gear Club Unlimited 3, and Edge of Memories have been canceled. These releases were slated for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X.

The games are still in development, but Video Games Plus has confirmed that all ESRB physical copies of the games have been canceled and will not be released. They’re offering refunds for any customers who placed pre-orders with them.

NACON Has Struggled Financially Recently

NACON recently announced it was at risk of closure after filing for an emergency insolvency. The company then announced it would delay its Connect event to focus more on the games in development. In their statement, they said they had many projects that were still in development and announced that releases remain on track. Check out the full statement here.

The connect event was originally scheduled for March 4 and is now expected to be held in May.

Insider Gaming will continue to report any and all updates on that situation, as well as news regarding NACON.

In other physical release news, Nintendo recently announced that first-party titles published on the Nintendo Switch 2 will have different MSRP between their digital and physical releases. Learn more about that here.

How do you feel about this news concerning NACON? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.
